2015-12-01.title: Narendra Modi Could Make or Break Obama’s Climate Legacy

2015-12-03.abstract: Through various philanthropies, Monsignor Albert established centers to provide food, education, health care and other services for drug addicts and people with H.I.V.

2015-12-25.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: To Londoners’ surprise, the holiday was a traditional one, at least as far as external temperature was concerned.

2015-12-25.title: Christmas Messages Look Back on a 2015 of Upheaval
2015-12-25.abstract: Excerpts of sermons and messages from Pope Francis and other members of the clergy from across the globe.

2015-12-26.title: Turkish President Erdogan Dissuades Man From Jumping Off Bridge
2015-12-26.abstract: The president, known for his sharp rebuttals and rejoinders, used his skills to save a man who contemplated jumping off the Bosporus Bridge.

2015-12-26.title: Iran: Relatives Visit a Jailed Reporter
2015-12-26.abstract: Jason Rezaian, the Washington Post correspondent who has been incarcerated since July 2014, was permitted an extended visit with his wife and mother.

2015-12-26.title: Iraq: Leading Cleric Urges Release of Kidnapped Qatari Hunters
2015-12-26.abstract: Grand Ayatollah Ali al-Sistani called the kidnapping wrong and said the government must help free the abducted hunters.

2015-12-26.title: China Says It Will Expel French Journalist
2015-12-26.abstract: The reporter, Ursula Gauthier, had written an article criticizing Beijing’s attempt to link the Paris attacks to ethnic violence in China’s northwest.

2015-12-26.title: The Killing of Farkhunda
2015-12-26.abstract: Farkhunda Malikzada, a 27-year-old Muslim woman falsely accused of burning a Quran, was killed by a mob in central Kabul as hundreds watched and filmed. This video contains scenes of graphic violence.

2015-12-27.title: ISIS, Facing Losses, Releases Recording Said to Be of Leader
2015-12-27.abstract: In a recorded speech, whose authenticity could not be confirmed, Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi implored more Muslims to fight on behalf of his group.

2015-12-27.title: Drought Deepens South Africa’s Malaise
2015-12-27.abstract: The full impact of the drought and resulting poor harvests will be felt only in the months ahead, but they have already left 29 million people without reliable access to food, the U.N. says.

2015-12-27.title: Veteran International Prosecutor Foresees War Crimes Trials for ISIS
2015-12-27.abstract: Stephen Rapp said it would be “unthinkable” to do without a process of justice in the future for any peace agreement to have the support of the Syrian people.

2015-12-27.title: China Passes Antiterrorism Law That Critics Fear May Overreach
2015-12-27.abstract: Opponents who had seen a draft version said it grants broad new powers that could be abused to monitor peaceful citizens and steal commercial secrets.

2015-12-27.title: 1940: England Seeks U.S. Accord on Orient Defense
2015-12-27.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: The British look for the United States to take effective action in the Pacific region in 1940.

2015-12-28.title: Argentina’s New President Moves Swiftly to Shake Up the Economy
2015-12-28.abstract: Mauricio Macri’s economic changes are roiling Argentina, accentuating the divide he wanted to bridge and leading some Argentines to doubt whether he will be a change for the better.

2015-12-28.title: Tied Vote on Catalan Separatist Leader in Spain
2015-12-28.abstract: An election to let Artur Mas, who lost a crucial vote last month, serve another term as the head of a coalition of parties supporting Catalonia’s secession yielded a deadlock on Sunday.

2015-12-28.title: Obama’s ‘Boots on the Ground’: U.S. Special Forces Are Sent to Tackle Global Threats
2015-12-28.abstract: While President Obama has said he opposes sending Americans to fight in far-flung countries, his administration carves out exceptions for Special Operations.

2015-12-28.title: Remembering Alan Kurdi: Syrian Family’s Tragedy Goes Beyond Image of Boy on Beach
2015-12-28.abstract: The death of Alan Kurdi, 2, which focused the world on refugees, was just one chapter of a sprawling family’s story in its desperate attempts to escape Syria and find a new home.

2015-12-28.title: As China Hungers for Coal, ‘Behemoth’ Studies the Ravages at the Source
2015-12-28.abstract: Zhao Liang’s film “Behemoth” depicts the harmful effects of mining on Inner Mongolia’s grasslands, and its people.

2015-12-29.title: Sudan Is a Waypoint on East Africans’ Trek to Europe
2015-12-29.abstract: Thousands of migrants, especially from Eritrea and Ethiopia, reach Sudan every year hoping to earn money to pay a smuggler for passage to Libya and, ultimately, Europe.

2015-12-29.title: Suspected Boko Haram Attacks Kill Scores in Nigeria
2015-12-29.abstract: At least 52 people were killed and 124 injured in bombings and shootings in the city of Maiduguri.

2015-12-29.title: Iran: Deaths From Swine Flu Rise
2015-12-29.abstract: Deaths from a swine flu outbreak in Iran have more than tripled in the past three weeks to at least 112.

2015-12-29.title: Evacuations Aim to Ease Path to Talks in Syria War
2015-12-29.abstract: Several hundred wounded militants and their families, from opposing sides of the war, were evacuated from besieged areas on Monday in the latest attempt to strike local agreements to calm the conflict.

2015-12-29.title: Maritime ‘Repo Men’: A Last Resort for Stolen Ships
2015-12-29.abstract: Thousands of boats are stolen each year, and some are recovered using alcohol, prostitutes, witch doctors and other forms of guile.

2015-12-30.title: Burkina Faso: First New Leader in Decades Is Sworn In
2015-12-30.abstract: The inauguration of Roch Marc Christian Kaboré drew officials from eight other West African countries and thousands of supporters.

2015-12-30.title: Egypt Shuts Arts Venues Amid Signs of Clampdown
2015-12-30.abstract: The Egyptian authorities have shut down a pioneering art gallery and raided an independent publishing house in what activists say is a move to muffle dissent ahead of the January 25 anniversary of 2011 uprising.

2015-12-30.title: Argentina: Ex-Transportation Ministers Get Prison
2015-12-30.abstract: Juan Pablo Schiavi, who held the post at the time, was given an eight-year sentence, convicted of causing the deaths of 51 people in a 2012 train crash in Buenos Aires.

2015-12-30.title: Aide to Kim Jong-un Dies in Car Accident, North Korean Media Says
2015-12-30.abstract: Kim Yang-gon, 73, head of the United Front Department of the Workers’ Party of Korea, was a key foreign policy aide for the country’s young and relatively inexperienced leader.

2015-12-30.title: Shooting in Dagestan, Focus of ISIS Recruiters, Leaves One Dead
2015-12-30.abstract: A border guard was killed and 10 were wounded on the observation deck of an ancient citadel in Derbent, according to Russia’s Investigative Committee.

2015-12-31.title: Egypt Officials Stop Facebook Program for Free Access to Internet
2015-12-31.abstract: The program, Free Basics, began in October and had drawn more than three million Egyptians.

2015-12-31.title: Climate Chaos, Across the Map
2015-12-31.abstract: With tornado outbreaks in the South, Christmas temperatures that sent trees into bloom in Central Park and historic floods in England, 2015 is closing with a string of weather anomalies.

2015-12-31.title: Caetano Veloso Loves Israel, but Won’t Be Going Back
2015-12-31.abstract: One of Brazil’s most revered singers says his stance on the Israeli-Palestinian conflict has been widely misunderstood.

2015-12-31.title: Venezuelan Court Blocks 4 Lawmakers
2015-12-31.abstract: The move, in response to a challenge from a candidate in the governing Socialist party, blocked three from the opposition and one Socialist just days before a new National Assembly was to be sworn in.

2015-12-31.title: Britain: 2 Plotters Sentenced
2015-12-31.abstract: The sentences were for a divorced couple convicted of plotting a terrorist attack in London on the 10th anniversary of the transit bombings there.
